# **Research Progress** 

**Project Title: ProteoPicking\
Student Name(s): Lina Maltrovsky\
Supervisor/Lab: Ada Junquera\
Start Date: 09.09.24\
Expected Completion Date: 31.03.25**

**\
GitHub Repository (if applicable):**\
[[https://github.com/farkkilab/ProteoPick_LinasThesis]{.underline}](https://github.com/farkkilab/ProteoPick_LinasThesis)

**Shared Folder Link:**
[[https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1FikgMgnoSuW8eaZljcNtUKKxV28b12tn?usp=sharing]{.underline}](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1FikgMgnoSuW8eaZljcNtUKKxV28b12tn?usp=sharing)

## **Project Overview**

**Brief Description of Research Goals:\
**In this project, we want to establish a protocol for a new method that
combines highly multiplexed imaging with proteomics analysis. For this
we are using the picking function of the RarecyteFinder to isolate
specific parts of the t-CycIF stained tissue. The cell samples are then
sent to a collaboration partner for the proteomics analysis.

Moreover, an analysis pipeline for the analysis of the data that is
collected during ProteoPicking experiments was created during this
project. It analyses image data & proteomics data and in the integrates
results from both parts of the project

Image Analysis:

\- Illumination Correction

\- Ashlar Stitching

\- Segmentation (Stardist, Mesmer, Cellpose; different image filters)

\- Quantification

\- Phenotyping

roject

### **Step 1: Proteopick Pilot (4_5)**

- **Description:**\
 > establishing if the general concept works

 - Does t-CycIF staining affect protein content of tissue?

 - How many picks are necessary for usable results?

 - Which buffer should be used - Urea or TFE?

 - Is there a difference in the T/S protein content?

- **Date Started:** 17.09.24

- **Expected Completion Date:**

 - Experimental Completion Date: 27.09.24

 - Arrival of Proteomics Results: 30.10.24

- **Files/Links:**

 - Experimental plan:

- **Challenges Encountered:**

 - Problems with the picking process - were resolved with help of
 > customer service

- **Notes:**

 - tCycIF and proteomics analysis can be combined - nearly no
 > difference between IF stained protein content and tCycIF
 > stained protein content

 - necessary picks: 5,10 and 20 picks resulted in acceptable
 > results

 - Urea and TFE are both fine - since Urea is less toxic we will
 > continue using Urea

 - Proteomics results showed contamination with a kind of polymer
 > and keratin (probably due to dust/microdust)

### **Step 2: Proteopick Optimization (4_6)**

- **Description:**\
 > improvement of already established protocol & obtaining biological
 > insight

 - getting rid of contaminations by changes in the protocol

 - using 7 picks with thicker sections

 - comparing picks from tumor core, tumor-stroma interface and
 > stroma

- **Challenges Encountered:**

 - Some of the sectioned slides detached after the antigen
 > retrieval step -- and even after resectioning the problem
 > remained\
 > solution: choosing different sample blocks

 - There were no Ki67+ stroma cells\
 > solution: we picked p21- cells as non-senescent stroma cells
 > and p21+ cells as senescent stroma cells

 - Finding tumor core ROIs was sometimes difficult since some tumor
 > parts were very small and therefore all the cells were close
 > to stroma cells\

as senescent stroma cells

 - Finding tumor core ROIs was sometimes difficult since some tumor
 > parts were very small and therefore all the cells were close
 > to stroma cells\
 > solution: in these cases we chose the best that was possible,
 > but maybe some of the tumor spots could be reevaluated as
 > tumor-stroma-interface

 - During the picking process there were initially some problems,
 > but we found a solution to pick with just one additional
 > step - clicking on init or touch before clicking on pick

- **Challenges Encountered:**

 - some channels do not overlap perfectly - problems with
 > stitching? → no

 - segmentation with stardist did not work -\
 > sections are thicker than normal (10µm instead of 4µm),
 > therefore there are a lot of clustered, overlapping cells that
 > stardist was not able to mask\
 > → testing of other segmentation methods and image enhancing
 > methods

 - Created mesmer segmentation code cannot segment full whole slide
 > images - image needs to be tiled and tiles need to be
 > segmented separately before the segmented tiles are stitched
 > back together

ion code cannot segment full whole slide
 > images - image needs to be tiled and tiles need to be
 > segmented separately before the segmented tiles are stitched
 > back together

 - Created tiling code does not work flawlessly - segments tissue,
 > but also segments background noise at the black edges of the
 > images\
 > → instead of using the Tiling-Mesmer-Untiling code, we stick
 > to the mesmer code without tiling that only segments smaller
 > images and segment only smallcrops of the picks

 - Phenotyping with SciMap was challenging - an error in the SciMap
 > scaling function source code was found and a solution was
 > found for this by changing parts of the source code
